summary: High‑quality soundboard capture that shines on the Tighten Up Jam within Dancing in the Street and delivers a laid‑back, unique vibe throughout.
review: The recording is praised as great‑quality and essentially flawless, likely coming from a soundboard source. Fans highlight the “Tighten Up Jam” embedded in “Dancing in the Street” and note an overall relaxed, almost mystical atmosphere that has kept this show on their top shelf for decades. While Bob Weir’s early excitement is mentioned and the NRPS material feels a bit flat, the performance is generally regarded as a standout vintage Dead experience.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews are favorable, emphasizing strong musical moments and good recording quality; with limited quantity of reviews confidence is medium.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers describe the audio as great‑quality and crisp; no specific issues mentioned, indicating a clean and listenable mix.

standout_songs: Dancing in the Street (Tighten Up Jam)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows with a relaxed, laid‑back vibe; Bob Weir’s early enthusiasm tapers into a smoother groove, and despite some flat NRPS moments the overall pacing is praised.

band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby (Bob Weir) sounds 'excited' in the early part of the set then calms later.
